@anthropic-ai/sdk.omission_reason: messages.batches.retrieve() is an idempotent polling GET with no distinct error contract beyond the standard APIError hierarchy already covered by batches.create(). messages.batches.results() is a streaming JSONL download — same error profile as retrieve, plus network streaming errors identical to messages.stream() already contracted. beta.files.delete/download/list/retrieveMetadata are admin or read-only operations rarely called in SaaS app runtime code. models.retrieve/models.list are read-only GETs with no meaningful domain-specific error contracts. completions.create() is the deprecated legacy Text Completions API — Anthropic explicitly recommends migrating to Messages API and future models will not support it.
@anthropic-ai/sdk.notes: 4 new functions contracted: countTokens (2 postconditions: no-try-catch + estimate-as-exact), batches.create (3 postconditions: no-try-catch + result-not-polled + individual-errors-not-handled), beta.files.upload (2 postconditions: no-try-catch + id-not-validated), messages.parse (2 postconditions: no-try-catch + null-output-unchecked). 4 scanner concerns queued (concern-20260402-anthropic-sdk-deepen-1 through -4). Key new insights: (1) countTokens has independent rate limits separate from messages.create; (2) batches produce individual result statuses (succeeded/errored/canceled/expired) that must be checked per-item; (3) beta.files.upload has storage quota errors (403) distinct from standard HTTP errors; (4) messages.parse returns parsed_output as T | null — callers must null-check before use.

functions_intentionally_omitted: GetBucket* (ACL, CORS, Encryption, IntelligentTiering, Inventory, LifecycleConfiguration, Location, Logging, MetricsConfiguration, NotificationConfiguration, OwnershipControls, Policy, PolicyStatus, Replication, RequestPayment, Tagging, Versioning, Website)

PutBucket* (AccelerateConfiguration, ACL, CORS, Encryption, IntelligentTiering, Inventory, LifecycleConfiguration, Logging, MetricsConfiguration, NotificationConfiguration, OwnershipControls, Policy, Replication, RequestPayment, Tagging, Versioning, Website)

DeleteBucket* (AnalyticsConfiguration, CORS, Encryption, IntelligentTiering, InventoryConfiguration, Lifecycle, MetricsConfiguration, OwnershipControls, Policy, Replication, Tagging, Website, PublicAccessBlock)
GetObject* (ACL, Attributes, LegalHold, LockConfiguration, Retention, Tagging, Torrent)
@aws-sdk/client-s3.omission_reason: Admin/config GET/PUT/DELETE commands for bucket configuration (CORS, encryption, lifecycle, ACL, etc.) are infrastructure management operations rarely called in app code. Object tagging, versioning metadata, and legal hold operations are compliance admin ops. SelectObjectContentCommand (S3 Select) and WriteGetObjectResponseCommand (Object Lambda) are niche APIs. CreateSessionCommand is S3 Express One Zone — very new, limited adoption. All these have no unique behavioral contracts beyond generic HTTP error handling. ListParts/ListMultipartUploads/ListObjectVersions are pagination utilities covered by list-operation postcondition.
previously_contracted: send (object operations: GetObject, PutObject, DeleteObject, HeadObject, CopyObject)
@aws-sdk/client-s3.notes: 5 new function entries added this pass. Key insights: (1) DeleteObjectsCommand has a critical silent failure pattern — individual object deletion failures go to response.Errors[], not thrown, so code that doesn't check response.Errors silently loses data. (2) waitUntilObjectExists/waitUntilBucketExists throw TimeoutError (confirmed from @smithy/util-waiter source) — upload confirmation flows crash without try-catch. (3) AbortMultipartUploadCommand used in cleanup paths also throws NoSuchUpload — must be wrapped in its own try-catch. (4) RestoreObjectCommand throws ObjectAlreadyInActiveTierError (409) and GlacierExpeditedRetrievalNotAvailable (503). Scanner concern queued for response-pattern detection on DeleteObjectsCommand batch deletes.

@aws-sdk/client-ses.omission_reason: Read-only GETs (GetTemplateCommand, GetSendQuotaCommand, GetSendStatisticsCommand), idempotent identity verification operations (VerifyEmailIdentityCommand, VerifyDomainIdentityCommand) with no distinct errors beyond generic SESServiceException, and delete operations (DeleteTemplateCommand) that are admin/cleanup ops not typical in SaaS runtime code.
@aws-sdk/client-ses.notes: 4 new function entries added this pass. Key insights: (1) SendBounceCommand throws MessageRejected and has a 24-hour OriginalMessageId constraint — stale bounce queues silently lose DSN compliance; (2) UpdateConfigurationSetEventDestinationCommand has 5 distinct error types (CloudWatch/Firehose/SNS invalid config) making it the richest error contract in this pass; (3) PutConfigurationSetDeliveryOptionsCommand throws InvalidDeliveryOptionsException for invalid TlsPolicy values — relevant for TLS-enforcement compliance flows; (4) DeleteConfigurationSetCommand is NOT idempotent unlike S3 deletions — double-delete throws ConfigurationSetDoesNotExistException, a common cleanup script assumption bug.

@clerk/nextjs.omission_reason: clerkClient() is a factory method — contracted via its sub-methods. verifyToken() is a low-level JWT utility rarely called directly. getAuth() is the Pages Router equivalent of auth() with identical error profile. useAuth/useSession/useOrganization/useOrganizationList/useSessionList/useEmailLink/useWaitlist/useReverification/useOrganizationCreationDefaults are React hooks that return state objects — they do not perform async operations or throw. signIn.attemptSecondFactor has identical error profile to attemptFirstFactor. signUp.prepareVerification/attemptVerification share the error profile with signUp.create. clerkClient().users.updateUser/getUser have identical ClerkAPIResponseError profile to createUser. buildClerkProps/createRouteMatcher/createFrontendApiProxyHandlers/createClerkClient are sync factories or utilities. reverificationErrorResponse is a utility to create error responses.
@clerk/nextjs.notes: Pass 2: Added clerkFrontendApiProxy() with 3 postconditions covering env var misconfiguration (proxy-missing-publishable-key, proxy-missing-secret-key) and network failure (proxy-network-failure-returns-502). Key insight: clerkFrontendApiProxy returns error Response objects (500/502) instead of throwing — requires new detector patterns (env-var-presence, response-status-check) rather than try-catch detection. Also expanded omissions to include 10 additional React hooks and sync utilities that were not explicitly categorized in pass 1. 2 scanner concerns queued.

@elastic/elasticsearch.omission_reason: knnSearch is deprecated — replaced by knn option in search(). sql.query is the legacy SQL interface, rarely used in SaaS apps that have migrated to esql. helpers.msearch is an advanced batching helper with identical error profile to msearch() — covered by msearch contract. helpers.esql is a transformation wrapper around esql.query() — covered by esql.query contract. helpers.scrollDocuments has identical error profile to helpers.scrollSearch (both return AsyncIterable) — one contract covers both. ping() is a health-check utility with no business data impact.
@elastic/elasticsearch.notes: 6 new function entries added this pass. Key insights: (1) msearch() resolves HTTP 200 with ErrorResponseBase objects in responses[] for per-search failures — confirmed from MsearchResponseItem type in types.d.ts; (2) closePointInTime() and clearScroll() are resource-cleanup methods requiring finally-block placement; (3) reindex() has same partial-operation failure pattern as deleteByQuery/updateByQuery (response.failures[]); (4) esql.query() syntax errors manifest as runtime ResponseError(400); (5) helpers.scrollSearch() uses async iterator — for-await-of loop needs enclosing try-catch. 6 scanner concerns queued.

@prisma/client.omission_reason: findMany/count/aggregate/groupBy are read-only operations that return empty arrays/0 (not null/throw), with no meaningful domain-specific error contracts beyond generic connection errors. $queryRawUnsafe/$executeRawUnsafe are explicitly unsafe variants that share the same error space as $queryRaw/$executeRaw but the developer has already opted in to unsafe mode. createManyAndReturn/updateManyAndReturn are convenience variants of createMany/updateMany with identical error profiles.

@slack/web-api.omission_reason: conversations.info/members/replies and chat.getPermalink are read-only GETs with no error contracts beyond generic HTTP. users.profile.get is a read-only GET. views.push/publish/update have identical error profiles to views.open which is contracted. oauth.v2.access is an OAuth flow method rarely called in user app code (handled by framework/middleware).

@supabase/supabase-js.omission_reason: channel/removeChannel/removeAllChannels are Realtime subscription event-based APIs with no meaningful throw/return error contracts (they use callbacks). schema() is a builder method with no own errors. signInAnonymously is rarely used in SaaS apps (typically disabled). resend() and updateUser() have identical error profiles to signUp/signInWithPassword already contracted.

ai.omission_reason: experimental_generateVideo is an experimental API with limited real-world adoption and no provider implementations in the corpus repos. NoVideoGeneratedError follows the identical pattern as NoImageGeneratedError — same error class hierarchy, same retry logic.

algoliasearch.omission_reason: deleteIndex is an admin/teardown op rarely called in runtime SaaS code (and non-existent index deletion is silently ignored). browse/browseObjects/browseRules/browseSynonyms are export/admin operations rarely called in runtime SaaS code — same error profile as search(). chunkedBatch is an internal helper with identical error profile to saveObjects. API key management (addApiKey/deleteApiKey/updateApiKey/listApiKeys) are admin ops that belong in setup scripts, not runtime application code. Index management (listIndices/listClusters/operationIndex) are admin ops. Rule/synonym management (saveRule/saveRules/clearRules/saveSynonym/saveSynonyms/clearSynonyms) are setup ops. batch() is the low-level primitive beneath saveObjects/deleteObjects — all covered by higher-level helper contracts.
algoliasearch.notes: All 4 new functions are v5 client-direct API methods. Ground-truth fixtures use `as any` cast (same limitation as other v5 methods). Scanner concern queued for all 4 methods to add await_patterns. deleteBy unique behavior: explicitly rejects empty filters (ApiError 400) — important for GDPR erasure workflows.

axios.omission_reason: options() is almost never called directly by application code — browsers issue OPTIONS requests automatically as CORS preflight, and Node.js server code rarely uses OPTIONS explicitly. Its error profile is identical to get/delete/head.

bcrypt.omission_reason: All 7 bcrypt API functions are contracted. No functions omitted.

bullmq.omission_reason: Worker.waitUntilReady() is a test utility. Worker.getNextJob() is an advanced manual-fetching pattern. FlowProducer.getFlow() is a read-only getter. FlowProducer.close()/QueueEvents.run()/QueueEvents.close() have identical error profiles to already-contracted Queue.close()/Worker.run(). Queue.remove()/removeJobScheduler() are cleanup ops with identical Redis error profile to add(). Job.updateData()/updateProgress()/log()/promote()/changeDelay()/changePriority() all share identical Redis error profiles with no domain-specific contracts beyond generic connection errors. Job.extendLock()/moveToFailed() are internal worker operations rarely called by application code.
bullmq.notes: 13 new function entries added this pass. Key insights: (1) Worker.run() requires error listener attached BEFORE start — missing listener causes Node.js unhandled error crash, explicitly warned in BullMQ docs; (2) FlowProducer.add() is atomic all-or-nothing via Redis MULTI/EXEC — partial flow creation is impossible but Redis failure loses entire flow silently; (3) upsertJobScheduler() failure during deployment means zero recurring jobs until next successful registration — silent cron death; (4) obliterate() throws on active jobs unless force=true — SCAN-based iteration on large queues can fail mid-obliteration; (5) Job.waitUntilFinished() rejects on both timeout AND job failure — commonly used in request-response patterns where unhandled rejection hangs HTTP requests. 4 scanner concerns queued: FlowProducer instance tracking, Worker.run() error-listener detection, Queue method await_patterns, Job instance method tracking.

functions_intentionally_omitted: Malformed urlencoded form (internal error - very rare, covered by busboy-001 error handler)
busboy.omission_reason: Malformed urlencoded form error propagated via stream write callback (cb(new Error(...))) — this is an internal stream mechanism covered generically by busboy-001 error handler. Not worth a separate postcondition as it only occurs with extremely malformed percent-encoding that no real-world client would send.
busboy.notes: 4 new postconditions added this pass. Key insights: (1) busboy-006 (field.valueTruncated) — the 1MB default fieldSize limit is commonly exceeded by JSON blobs in text fields; silent truncation is a DATA_LOSS risk; (2) busboy-007 (Unexpected end of form) — most common production busboy error, occurs on client disconnect/mobile network switch; covered by busboy-001 error handler but now explicitly documented with specific error messages; (3) busboy-008 (Multipart boundary not found) — third distinct constructor throw, occurs when Content-Type is multipart/form-data but boundary parameter is absent; covered by busboy-002 try/catch; (4) busboy-009 (field.nameTruncated) — 100-byte field name limit silently truncates dynamic field names; INFO severity as impact is limited to unusual use cases.

cassandra-driver.omission_reason: executeGraph() is the DSE Graph API, not Cassandra CQL — not present in SaaS app codebases. getReplicas()/getState() are read-only metadata helpers with no error contracts beyond connection failure already covered by connect(). Mapper.batch() is an advanced multi-model batch API rarely called in SaaS code, with identical error profile to ModelMapper operations. ModelMapper.findAll() and mapWithQuery() have identical error profiles to find() which is already contracted.
cassandra-driver.notes: 6 new function entries added this pass covering Mapper ORM layer and executeConcurrent bulk op. Key insight: executeConcurrent() has dual error behavior — raiseOnFirstError=true (default) throws on first error, raiseOnFirstError=false silently accumulates errors in result.errors[] up to maxErrors. 3 scanner concerns queued: mapper-get-no-try-catch (ModelMapper.get() not recognized), mapper-get-null-not-checked (null return type analysis needed), execute-concurrent-errors-not-checked (option-argument-aware result check analysis needed). 3 of 14 SHOULD_FIRE ground-truth tests currently failing due to missing scanner detectors (acceptable per Phase 4 rules).

cloudinary.omission_reason: remove_all_tags/remove_all_context/replace_tag share identical error profiles with already-contracted add_tag/remove_tag (same execute_request.js codepath, same HTTP codes, same plain object error shape). No additional behavioral contracts needed.
cloudinary.notes: 3 new functions contracted: update_metadata (2 postconditions: missing-error-handling + silent-partial-failure when public_ids do not exist), api.update (1 postcondition: missing-error-handling for 404/401/403/420), api.delete_resources_by_tag (2 postconditions: missing-error-handling + catastrophic-misuse for scope bleed). Key insights: (1) update_metadata silently omits non-existent public_ids from result instead of throwing 404; (2) api.update targets Admin API — needs api_key+api_secret, not just upload key; (3) delete_resources_by_tag is irreversible bulk delete — scope verification via resources_by_tag() is critical before calling.

drizzle-orm.omission_reason: selectDistinct/selectDistinctOn have identical error profile to select() — same connection errors, same constraint errors. refreshMaterializedView() is a maintenance operation rarely called in SaaS app code. $count() returns 0 on empty result (not null/throw) with no meaningful domain-specific error contract beyond connection error already covered by select.

express.omission_reason: express.raw() and express.text() are built-in body parsing middleware with identical error profile (entity.too.large, charset.unsupported, entity.parse.failed) to express.json() — same underlying body-parser/raw-body code path. app.param() uses the same async callback pattern as app.use() middleware — errors from async param callbacks propagate identically (unhandled rejection in Express 4, auto-caught in Express 5). Already covered by async-middleware-unhandled-rejection postcondition.
express.notes: 7 new function entries added this pass. Key insights: (1) express.json() errors (SyntaxError 400, PayloadTooLarge 413) come from body-parser/raw-body with specific error.type values (entity.parse.failed, entity.too.large, charset.unsupported) — must be caught by 4-param error middleware; (2) res.sendFile/download use pillarjs/send which maps fs errors: ENOENT->404, ENAMETOOLONG->404, other->500, dotfile->403; (3) app.listen() delegates to http.Server.listen() — EADDRINUSE is the #1 Express startup failure; (4) res.render() errors depend on the template engine but Express wraps them in a standard callback(err, html) pattern. 5 scanner concerns queued: 3 uncovered functions (sendFile, download, render) + 2 false positives (express.json sync factory registration, app.listen with server.on error handler).

firebase.omission_reason: Auth: applyActionCode/checkActionCode are email action handlers rarely called in SaaS code. fetchSignInMethodsForEmail deprecated for security (email enumeration). getRedirectResult used only for OAuth redirect flows (browser-only). linkWith*/reauthenticateWith* are edge cases for account linking. signInAnonymously rarely used in SaaS. signInWithCredential/CustomToken/EmailLink/PhoneNumber/Redirect are alternative sign-in methods with same error profile. Firestore: clearIndexedDbPersistence/disableNetwork/enableNetwork/waitForPendingWrites/terminate are offline mode management ops. getCountFromServer/getAggregateFromServer are read-only analytics queries. Storage: deleteObject/getBytes/getBlob/listAll/list/updateMetadata/uploadString share identical error profiles with uploadBytes/getDownloadURL already contracted.
firebase.notes: Scanner gap: deleteUser() not detected by current scanner (no await_pattern). All other 11 new functions detected correctly in ground-truth tests.

firebase-admin.omission_reason: getUserByPhoneNumber/getUserByEmail/getUsers share identical error profile with getUser. listUsers/deleteUsers are batch variants of single ops. sendEachForMulticast has same surface as sendEach. generateEmailVerificationLink shares surface with generatePasswordResetLink. unsubscribeFromTopic shares surface with subscribeToTopic. AppCheck.createToken/verifyToken rarely called directly in SaaS backends.
firebase-admin.notes: Depth pass 2026-04-06 (deepen-stream-2): Added 9 functions covering Auth session cookies, custom tokens, token revocation, password reset, Firestore transactions/batches, and modern messaging APIs. Ground-truth tests: 44/44 pass. Scanner correctly detects all 9 new function types via await_patterns.

functions_intentionally_omitted: pathExists (never throws — catches all errors internally, returns false on any error)
emptyDir (self-handles readdir errors, creates dir if missing)
ensureLink (niche filesystem op, rare in SaaS)
ensureSymlink (niche filesystem op, rare in SaaS)
fs-extra.key_findings: writeJson is a critical footgun — unlike outputFile and outputJson, it does NOT create parent directories. ENOENT when parent is missing is common. Also: remove() force:true only silences ENOENT, not EACCES/EPERM — silent failures in cleanup code.
scanner_concerns_resolved: concern-20260403-fs-extra-1 (mkdirp/mkdirs detection — scanner already detects these)
concern-20260403-fs-extra-2 (createFile detection — scanner already detects this)
fs-extra.notes: 3 new function entries added: mkdirp, mkdirs, createFile — all are aliases for existing contracted functions. mkdirp and mkdirs are aliases for ensureDir (same makeDir function reference, confirmed from lib/mkdirs/index.js). createFile is an alias for ensureFile (same function reference, confirmed from lib/ensure/file.js). Ground-truth tests: all 51 pass including 9 new SHOULD_FIRE annotations for the alias names. Scanner upgrade concerns 1 and 2 marked resolved — scanner already detects mkdirp/mkdirs/createFile. Coverage: 0.82 → 1.0 (12/12 non-omitted functions contracted).

ioredis.omission_reason: scan/hscan/sscan/zscan are cursor-based streaming operations — they are used via scanStream() helper in ioredis which wraps the low-level cursor. Contracting the low-level cursor commands would duplicate coverage. xadd/xread are Redis Streams commands with very limited SaaS adoption — rarely called in corpus repos.

jsonwebtoken.omission_reason: All 3 callable functions are contracted. No omissions.
jsonwebtoken.notes: decode() contract is unique: not error handling, but security invariant. jwt.decode() never throws (returns null), but using its return value for authentication without verify() is a complete auth bypass.

mongodb.omission_reason: estimatedDocumentCount/distinct are read-only with identical error profile to countDocuments. rename/dropCollection/dropDatabase/createCollection are admin/schema migration ops rarely called in SaaS app runtime code. dropIndexes has no distinct error contract vs dropIndex. createIndexes is a batch variant with identical error profile. indexExists is a read-only check. command() is too generic. withSession() is a convenience wrapper with same error profile as withTransaction. listDatabases/listCollections are read-only admin ops.
mongodb.notes: Scanner gap: findOneAndUpdate/findOneAndDelete/findOneAndReplace require Collection param tracking. watch() requires .on(error) listener detection not try-catch. close() lifecycle tracking requires new detection pattern. 6 concerns queued.

mongoose.omission_reason: createCollection/dropCollection/dropDatabase are admin DDL ops rarely called in SaaS app code paths; createSearchIndex/dropSearchIndex/updateSearchIndex/listSearchIndexes are Atlas-only and niche; Aggregate.explain() is diagnostic/read-only with no destructive error contracts
mongoose.notes: 5 new function entries added this pass covering insertOne/startSession/withSession/init/syncIndexes. Key insights: (1) insertOne() is a Mongoose 8.x addition that wraps $save() — same error surface as create() including ValidationError and E11000; (2) startSession() calls _waitForConnect() before returning — serverless cold start timing causes failures; (3) withSession() does NOT abort transactions on error — callers must manually abort if they use session.startTransaction() inside; (4) init() errors are silently swallowed in background (Model.init().catch(() => {})) — explicit callers must add their own try-catch; (5) syncIndexes() has a SyncIndexesError type (lib/error/syncIndexes.js) and is non-atomic — partial failures leave DB in mixed state. 5 scanner concerns queued.

functions_intentionally_omitted: useSession (React hook — renders with status field, no null/throw pattern to detect)
getCsrfToken (internal utility — returns string|undefined, no crash risk)
getProviders (returns provider list or null — low severity, no crash risk)
next-auth.omission_reason: useSession is a React hook that returns { data, status } — consumers check status field, not a null pattern the scanner detects. getCsrfToken and getProviders are rarely called directly and have no crash-level failure modes.

node-fetch.omission_reason: response.buffer() is deprecated (alias for arrayBuffer(), removed in node-fetch v4). response.formData() parses multipart forms — same consumeBody() error chain as text/blob/arrayBuffer but extremely rarely called directly in SaaS codebases; coverage is identical to arrayBuffer contract.

nodemailer.omission_reason: getTestMessageUrl() is a sync utility that returns false on failure, never throws. close() is sync teardown. isIdle() is sync boolean. use() is sync plugin registration. None have async error contracts.
nodemailer.notes: coverage_score = 4/4 contracted async-callable functions (100%). createTestAccount() makes HTTP call to Ethereal API with 3 error modes: network error, API error, JSON parse error.

openai.omission_reason: Read-only GETs (models.list/retrieve) have no meaningful error contracts beyond generic HTTP. images.createVariation and images.edit are rarely used. beta.assistants/threads are deprecated Assistants API. responses.retrieve/delete are cleanup ops. containers.create is experimental with no documented error contracts.

pg.omission_reason: copyFrom()/copyTo() are streaming bulk COPY operations for data migrations and ETL workloads. They are almost never called in SaaS application code — they require a writable/readable stream pipe chain and are used for bulk loading, not regular queries.

pino.omission_reason: Synchronous log methods (error/warn/info/debug/trace/silent) have no async error contracts — errors from serializers are covered by the serializer-must-not-throw edge case on pino(). setBindings()/bindings()/isLevelEnabled() are sync utility methods with no error contracts.
pino.notes: 4 new function entries added this pass covering the transport/destination/flush/multistream factory API. Key insights: (1) pino.transport() errors are non-recoverable per docs — process MUST terminate on error event; (2) pino.destination() wraps sonic-boom which emits 'error' events (not throws) on disk/permission failures; (3) logger.flush() callback error silently swallowed if not checked — common pattern in graceful shutdown code causes log loss; (4) pino.multistream() write() has no try-catch — individual stream failures propagate and can abort writes to all streams. 3 scanner concerns queued.

functions_intentionally_omitted: categoriesGet — read-only, no auth required, no item-level errors
institutionsGet — read-only lookup, no user-specific error surface
institutionsGetById — read-only GET, narrow error surface (404 only)
plaid.omission_reason: Read-only institutional lookups with no user-item-specific error contracts. Cannot throw ITEM_LOGIN_REQUIRED or TRANSFER_ERROR. Generic HTTP errors only.

puppeteer.omission_reason: $$eval has identical error profile to $eval. hover/tap/focus/select have identical error profile to click (element not found). $/$$  return null/empty array (no throws). setViewport/emulate/setJavaScriptEnabled/setBypassCSP are configuration methods with no meaningful error contracts. setCookie/deleteCookie/cookies are low-level cookie ops. authenticate/setExtraHTTPHeaders are configuration. bringToFront/addScriptTag/addStyleTag are utility. setGeolocation/waitForFileChooser are niche. createBrowserContext/browser.pages/browser.disconnect are admin/utility operations.
puppeteer.notes: 10 new function entries added this pass. Key insights: (1) page.evaluate() is the #1 cause of silent data loss in scraping — TargetCloseError when page navigates during eval; (2) page.pdf() only works in headless mode — headed throws immediately; (3) page.$eval() THROWS on missing element unlike $() which returns null — critical distinction; (4) page.waitForNetworkIdle() guaranteed to timeout on pages with WebSocket/SSE connections; (5) puppeteer.connect() failures crash browser pool workers if unhandled. 5 scanner concerns queued — all require Page instance tracking from puppeteer.launch() return chain.

redis.coverage_note: score corrected 2026-04-02: previous 0.63 was contracted/total; correct formula is contracted/(total-omitted) = 22/11 = 1.0. All non-omitted functions are contracted.
redis.omission_reason: mGet/mSet share identical error profile with get/set. decr/decrBy share identical profile with incr/incrBy. ttl/type/info/ping are read-only commands with no domain-specific error contracts. hDel/hmGet share error profile with hGet. lPop/rPop/rPush share error profile with lPush. sRem/sIsMember share profile with sAdd. zRem/zScore share profile with zAdd. disconnect() is synchronous (not async). pSubscribe/sSubscribe share profile with subscribe. scan/hscan/sscan/zscan are cursor-based admin operations rarely called in SaaS runtime code.
redis.notes: Scanner gaps: multi().exec() requires chained-method detection (concern-20260402-redis-deepen-1). subscribe() needs explicit await_pattern registration (concern-20260402-redis-deepen-2). publish() needs instance-tracking verification (concern-20260402-redis-deepen-3). Hash, list, set, sorted-set, counter, and expire operations all detected correctly via existing instance_tracking + await_patterns. 44 ground-truth tests all pass.

resend.omission_reason: emails.update/emails.create()/batch.create() are aliases or rare variants with identical error profiles to emails.send()/batch.send(). apiKeys.create/remove are admin ops rarely in SaaS app code. segments/domains creation are setup ops called once. broadcasts.create() is the draft-creation step with identical error profile to broadcasts.send(). contacts.remove/update/list are CRUD ops that share the same { data, error } pattern as contacts.create() — no additional postconditions needed beyond the base pattern.

stripe.omission_reason: update() operations are metadata changes with no meaningful domain-specific error contracts beyond generic HTTP. list()/search() are read-only GETs. expire() for checkout sessions is rarely called programmatically (sessions expire naturally). Coverage score is 9/13 total or ~100% of non-omitted callable functions.

functions_intentionally_omitted: superagent(url) callable-as-function — same error profile as get(), redundant contract
Request.end() callback — legacy callback pattern, same errors as get() but via first-arg callback
functions_uncovered: Request.pipe() — streaming errors may not surface as Promise rejections; requires event listener pattern
superagent.omission_reason: callable-as-function is identical to get(). end() callback is the legacy pre-Promise API with identical errors in first arg.

twilio.omission_reason: incomingPhoneNumbers.create() is a phone number provisioning/admin operation, not a runtime SaaS app code path. It's called once during setup, not in per-request code, and has the same error profile as the already-contracted messages.create() auth errors.

typeorm.omission_reason: Deprecated methods omitted (findByIds, exist, findOneById, connect, close). Thin-alias methods omitted (updateAll/deleteAll same as update/delete, preload same as findOne, sql tagged template same as query, findAndCountBy same as findAndCount, existsBy same as exists, softRemove/recover same error profile as remove). Aggregate math functions (sum/avg/min/max) omitted as connection failure only — no domain-specific contracts.

@sendgrid/mail.omission_reason: setClient/setTwilioEmailAuth/setTimeout/setSubstitutionWrappers are all synchronous void configuration methods with no async error contracts. They do not throw and return void.
@sendgrid/mail.notes: All async-callable functions (send, sendMultiple) are already contracted. setApiKey has a validation postcondition for whitespace trimming. 4 sync config methods intentionally omitted. No new functions added this pass — contract was already complete.

@upstash/redis.omission_reason: Administrative and maintenance commands (keys, scan, type, ttl, pttl, dump, restore, migrate, rename, sort, randomkey) share the same UpstashError/network error profile as already-contracted get(). List sub-commands (lpos, lset, lrem, llen, linsert, lmove) have identical error profiles to lpush/lrange already contracted. Hash sub-commands (hlen, hkeys, hvals, hmget, hmset) have identical error profiles to hget/hset. Sorted set sub-commands (zincrby, zcard, zrank, zscore) share the zadd/zrange error profile. Set sub-commands (scard, sinterstore, sunion, sdiff) share the sadd/smembers profile. Low-level ops (object, wait, copy, unlink, persist, pexpire, expireat, rename, lolwut) are rare in SaaS application code.
@upstash/redis.notes: Key gaps addressed this pass: (1) subscribe() errors are event-dispatched not thrown — requires .on('error') listener; (2) pipeline keepErrors mode silently returns errors instead of throwing; (3) evalsha() NOSCRIPT error requires fallback to eval(); (4) getdel() state ambiguity on network failure for one-time-use tokens.

groq-sdk.omission_reason: models.retrieve/list/delete are admin/read-only ops with no meaningful domain-specific error contracts beyond generic HTTP. batches.retrieve/list are polling/read-only GETs — identical error profile to batches.create. files.list/delete/content/info are admin or read-only operations with no distinct error contracts beyond the generic APIError hierarchy already covered by files.create.
groq-sdk.notes: 3 new functions contracted: batches.create (2 postconditions: no-try-catch + result-not-checked), files.create (2 postconditions: no-try-catch + id-not-used), batches.cancel (1 postcondition: no-try-catch). 4 scanner concerns queued. Key insight: batch API is two-step workflow (files.create then batches.create) and per-item JSONL result errors are silently buried in the results file.

scanner_concerns: FAILING: line 195 save() — instance method on variable returned from findByPk not detected. Scanner needs instance variable tracking for Model results.
FAILING: line 315 reload() — same issue: instance method on variable returned from findByPk not detected.
Instance methods validate() also at risk but passes because build() result is used inline in the same scope.
Chained model access: sequelize.models.User.bulkCreate() works because scanner tracks .models.X pattern already.

mailgun.js.omission_reason: Read-only GETs (events.get, suppressions.list/get, stats.*, metrics.*, logs.*, tags.list, webhooks.list/get, lists.list/get, lists.members.listMembers/getMember), pure admin operations (domains.*, ips.*, ip_pools.*, subaccounts.*, inboxPlacements.*, dkimManagement.*, routing rules), and low-adoption batch status operations (validate.multipleValidation.list/get/destroy, lists.validate/validationResult/cancelValidation).
mailgun.js.notes: 8 new functions contracted this pass (pass 4): suppressions.destroy (compliance-critical bounce re-enablement), suppressions.destroyAll (NEW in v12.8.0 2026-04-14 - catastrophic bulk delete), lists.update (list property changes), lists.destroy (permanent list deletion), lists.members.updateMember (unsubscribe compliance), lists.members.destroyMember (GDPR deletion compliance), webhooks.create and webhooks.update (event pipeline setup). Coverage reaches 100% of write-operation API surface. Key compliance risks: (1) suppressions.destroyAll is a new method that can wipe all bounce/unsubscribe lists with a single call - needs safeguard detection; (2) updateMember with subscribed:false and destroyMember are CAN-SPAM critical operations.

jsonschema.omission_reason: getSchema() is a read-only lookup with no throw contract. setSchemas() is a setup utility with no throw behavior. addSubSchemaArray() and addSubSchemaObject() are internal helpers not part of the public API surface — they do not appear in the TypeScript declarations and should not be called directly by application code.
jsonschema.notes: 5 new postconditions added. Key additions: (1) validate-invalid-schema-argument — SchemaError thrown when schema arg is null/non-object (confirmed from validator.js:107-109); (2) validate-unknown-attribute-throws — SchemaError when allowUnknownAttributes:false with unknown keywords (confirmed from validator.js:207-210); (3) validate-result-unchecked — silent failure when result not checked without throw options (confirmed from helpers.js:79-81, SILENT_FAILURE category); (4) validator-validate-unresolved-ref — SchemaError when  URI not registered (confirmed from validator.js:256-273); (5) scan-duplicate-conflicting-schema — Error thrown on conflicting schema IDs (confirmed from scan.js:32-37). Ground-truth test file created in nark. All 5 concerns already queued from previous tooling.

body-parser.omission_reason: bodyParser() is the deprecated combined JSON+urlencoded middleware. The official Express docs and body-parser README explicitly deprecate it in favor of calling bodyParser.json() and bodyParser.urlencoded() separately.
body-parser.notes: 21 new postconditions added across all 4 functions covering: entity.verify.failed (403 — verify callback throws), encoding.unsupported (415 — unsupported Content-Encoding), request.size.invalid (400 — Content-Length mismatch), stream.encoding.set (500 — req.setEncoding() conflict), stream.not.readable (500 — double body read), plus urlencoded-qs-depth-exceeded (400 — qs depth limit). All confirmed from lib/read.js source and official README errors section. 6 scanner concerns queued.

functions_intentionally_omitted: ipKeyGenerator (sync pure utility, no error contract)
MemoryStore direct usage (internal default store, users use external stores)
express-rate-limit.omission_reason: ipKeyGenerator() is a synchronous pure utility function with no async behavior or error contract. MemoryStore is the default internal store — users interact with the rate limiter through rateLimit() not MemoryStore directly.
express-rate-limit.notes: Previous deepen pass (deepen-stream-2, 2026-04-15) started but was interrupted before writing getKey/resetKey entries to contract.yaml. This pass (deepen-stream-1, pass-5) completed the work: (1) getKey() postconditions: get-key-store-unsupported (crashes on unhandled rejection if store lacks get()), get-key-store-error (raw store error propagates unlike middleware which has passOnStoreError); (2) resetKey() postconditions: reset-key-unprotected-endpoint (silent no-op when reset endpoint is itself rate-limited), reset-key-wrong-key-format (silent no-op when key format mismatches keyGenerator output); (3) Added 4 new postconditions to rateLimit() from error codes: invalid-ip-address (ERR_ERL_INVALID_IP_ADDRESS bypass), x-forwarded-for-without-trust-proxy (ERR_ERL_UNEXPECTED_X_FORWARDED_FOR), double-count (ERR_ERL_DOUBLE_COUNT), created-in-request-handler (ERR_ERL_CREATED_IN_REQUEST_HANDLER — silent bypass). 6 scanner concerns queued. deepen-stream-2 pass-5 added: rateLimit-invalid-store (TypeError thrown synchronously at startup when store missing required methods, confirmed from dist/index.cjs line 821-824) and async-callback-error-routes-to-handler (all async callbacks wrapped by handleAsyncErrors() — errors route to next(error), confirmed from dist/index.cjs line 828-834). 2 more scanner concerns queued (total: 8 for express-rate-limit).

discord.js.omission_reason: Client.fetchGuildPreview and fetchVoiceRegions are read-only GETs with no domain-specific error contracts. Guild setter methods (setAFKChannel, setBanner, etc.) are admin configuration operations rarely called in SaaS app runtime code. ShardingManager methods are infrastructure-level bot sharding not used in web apps. AutoModerationRule and GuildTemplate management are admin operations with no meaningful runtime error contracts beyond generic DiscordAPIError.
discord.js.notes: 10 new function entries added this pass. Key insights: (1) Webhook.send() is a critical pattern — bots storing webhook URLs in DBs will crash on send when tokens expire (10015 error). RateLimitError is a second concern for high-volume webhook senders. (2) GuildMember.createDM() throws 50007 when user DMs are disabled — extremely common for moderation bots doing pre-ban DMs. (3) GuildBanManager.remove() throws 10026 Unknown Ban on double-unban — common race condition in moderation UIs. (4) ThreadChannel.setArchived() throws 40060 on locked threads — auto-archiving bots crash on locked threads. (5) GuildChannel.delete/edit throw 50013 permissions and 10003 unknown channel. 2 scanner concerns queued for Webhook and GuildChannel detector disambiguation.

jszip.omission_reason: JSZipStreamHelper.accumulate() is internally called by JSZipObject.async() — its error profile is fully covered by the file-async-no-try-catch and file-async-unsupported-type postconditions on async(). Direct use of accumulate() is rare in SaaS app code; the contract for async() provides sufficient coverage for the underlying stream accumulation errors.
jszip.notes: 1 new function contracted this pass: JSZipObject.async() (2 postconditions: file-async-no-try-catch - decompression errors from pako on corrupted DEFLATE data that passes loadAsync() validation; file-async-unsupported-type - blob/uint8array type unavailable in Node.js). Key insight: a zip archive can pass loadAsync() validation yet have individually corrupted file entries that fail during .async() decompression. This is a critical gap in error handling since most developers assume successful loadAsync means the archive is safe to read. Scanner concerns queued for detecting .async() calls without try-catch on JSZipObject instances.

sharp.omission_reason: All 4 async-callable functions are now contracted. Synchronous builder methods (resize, rotate, jpeg, png, etc.) return Sharp instance and surface errors via toFile()/toBuffer() — covered by existing postconditions.
sharp.notes: Depth pass 2026-04-15 (deepen-stream-1, pass 8): Added 2 functions covering metadata() and stats(). API surface analysis: only 4 methods return Promise — toFile, toBuffer, metadata, stats. All other Sharp methods are synchronous builder-pattern configurators that queue processing but do not return Promises themselves; their errors surface via toFile()/toBuffer() which are already contracted. Key insights: (1) metadata() rejects with native Error from libvips VError — "Input file is missing: <path>", "Input file has corrupt header", "Input file contains unsupported image format", "Input image exceeds pixel limit" — all confirmed from src/common.cc:559-583; (2) stats() also rejects from libvips but requires FULL pixel decode, not just header read — corrupt pixel data that passes metadata() header check will still fail stats(); (3) critical correctness bug: stats() on a transformed image (e.g. after .extract(region)) reads stats from ORIGINAL, not the transformed result — requires intermediate .toBuffer() call.

functions_intentionally_omitted: TransactGetItemsCommand (same TransactionCanceledException contract as TransactWriteItemsCommand — documented in notes)
BatchExecuteStatementCommand (same silent partial failure pattern as BatchWriteItemCommand/BatchGetItemCommand — documented in notes)
@aws-sdk/client-dynamodb.omission_reason: TransactGetItemsCommand has identical TransactionCanceledException + CancellationReasons contract to TransactWriteItemsCommand — no new behavioral contract to add. BatchExecuteStatementCommand follows the exact same silent UnprocessedItems partial failure pattern as BatchWriteItemCommand and BatchGetItemCommand — no new postcondition needed. All DDL/admin commands (CreateTable, DeleteTable, DescribeTable, backup/import/export, Kinesis, tagging) are infrastructure management operations not called in SaaS app runtime code.
previously_contracted: send (covers GetItem, PutItem, DeleteItem, UpdateItem, QueryCommand, ScanCommand — broad postcondition for aws-dynamodb-service-error)
@aws-sdk/client-dynamodb.notes: 4 new function entries added this pass. Key insights: (1) BatchWriteItemCommand has critical silent partial failure — HTTP 200 with UnprocessedItems containing items that were NOT written; no exception thrown. This is the most dangerous DynamoDB silent failure pattern. (2) BatchGetItemCommand has symmetric silent partial failure — HTTP 200 with UnprocessedKeys containing items that could not be read; callers must retry until UnprocessedKeys is empty. (3) TransactWriteItemsCommand throws TransactionCanceledException with CancellationReasons array — callers must inspect each reason Code to distinguish ConditionalCheckFailed (business conflict, not retryable) from ProvisionedThroughputExceeded/TransactionConflict (infrastructure failures, retryable). (4) ExecuteStatementCommand PartiQL INSERT throws DuplicateItemException on existing primary key — unlike PutItemCommand which silently overwrites. 4 scanner concerns queued (concern-20260416-aws-dynamodb-deepen-1 through -4).
